response.justification: Bogdan is a skilled full-stack and distributed-systems engineer, but his profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role. The position demands deep, current C++ expertise as its core requirement, yet Bogdan's only documented C++ work is a six-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a university thesis project. His primary stack for the past decade is TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, and React. There is no evidence of industrial automation domain knowledge, IEC 62443 security standards, MQTT, or industrial protocols such as OPC UA or Modbus, all of which are central to this role.
strengths: Demonstrated experience building high-throughput, event-driven distributed systems handling millions of requests per second (Sociomantic/Dunnhumby role)
Hands-on with REST API design and asynchronous/concurrent programming patterns across multiple languages and platforms
Active community contributor through workshops, conference talks, and open-source projects, showing mentorship and communication ability
concerns: C++ experience is minimal (~6 months in 2014–2015) and entirely outdated relative to the role's requirement for deep, current C++/STL expertise
No industrial automation, IEC 62443, or industrial-protocol (MQTT, OPC UA, Modbus, DNP 3.0) experience anywhere in the resume
Secure Development Lifecycle (SDL) and industrial cybersecurity competencies are completely absent from the candidate's background

key_factors.factor: Industrial automation and security standards gap
